LHX.sentence: L3Harris has pulled back nearly 20% from its high, which for a defense contractor with steady government contracts is starting to look like an opportunity.

ASML.sentence: ASML dropped a bit but nothing has changed about their monopoly on the machines that make advanced computer chips — they're still the only game in town.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet day, though their profit margins aren't quite as strong as they used to be in the industrial gas business.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is down slightly but actually trading cheaper than other big tech stocks right now, even with all the AI buzz around Azure and ChatGPT.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is looking attractive after getting cheaper — the lab equipment giant is trading at lower prices while the life sciences industry keeps growing.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is up today and paying a solid dividend, though pipeline stocks aren't as attractive as they were when interest rates were lower.

ETHA.sentence: Ethereum dropped about 5% today and is sitting near the low end of its recent range — crypto has been in a consolidation phase.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore fell over 4% today as copper and other metals have been weak, though the long-term story for mining and commodity trading remains solid.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin dropped nearly 4% and is trading well below its recent highs — it's in a pullback after the big run-up earlier this year.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is holding steady — the Mexican peso is stable and Latin American soda demand keeps chugging along predictably.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is up nearly 2% with oil prices over $100, which typically means big dividend payments from Brazil's state oil company.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk is down slightly but trading below the replacement cost of their ships, which often signals the global shipping giant is undervalued.
